title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
summary: Lego's $200 Donkey Kong arcade machine designed by Carl Merriam has reportedly won approval from Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto. The working game set follows Lego's previous gaming nostalgia releases like the Game Boy and pinball machine.
content:
Carl Merriam has designed some of my favorite nostalgia-inducing Lego sets, including the Lego Nintendo Game Boy and Piranha Plant. He's assisted on the incredible Lion Knights' Castle, Galaxy Explorer, and Pirates of Barracuda Bay. 
Now, he's helped the company create a $200 Donkey Kong arcade machine set that managed to win even Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto's favor - or so he's told.
It doesn't play quite like Donkey Kong, of course. Jumpman, the character that would be renamed Mario, can never reach the top under his own power. But like Lego's $230 pinball machine, it does contain a basic fully functional game. There's a working joyst …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 乐高大金刚街机让马里奥无限跳跃——据传宫本茂很满意
zh.summary: 据报道，由卡尔·梅里亚姆设计的价值200美元的乐高大金刚街机获得了马里奥创造者宫本茂的批准。这款可工作的游戏套装延续了乐高之前的游戏怀旧产品，如Game Boy和弹球机。
